## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about a very special kind of webinar called a livecast. So livecasts are more like a TV broadcast, a talk show, than a traditional slide-based webinar. They can be done alone or they can be done with guests live on set or maybe online via Skype or Zoom. I've seen and you've probably seen these before where it's like a cast of several different experts and testimonial guests and those kinds of things all coming together in a broadcast that doesn't look like a bunch of slides being broadcast like a traditional webinar does, but faces are on camera and and they're they're in a set and they're talking about different things. That's what we mean when I talk about a livecast. I've actually seen a livecast at a quarter of a million dollars to a launch. In fact, I did one on my launch that actually increased the sales of my launch by about 35 percent and I've seen people succeed with replacing their webinar, their traditional webinar, all together with a livecast. In fact, if you go back to module three and I showed you the neuro gym livecast thank you page, that's actually a livecast with multiple guests on it that is running. It looks like it's actually shot in a TV set with these guests coming to visit like a traditional talk show. That's what a livecast looks and feels like plus a good livecast in addition to making you more money can create huge positive feelings within your audience and get substantially greater engagement in terms of things like watch time and comments and shares than a traditional webinar can. These can be extravagant multi hour events almost like a telethon kind of thing, but where you're selling rather than asking people to donate money. These can be have a set oftentimes and this is a hint having prizes on a regular basis valuable prizes that people really want that aren't the product other prizes around it or by the way another prize that you can put in there is some fun funny prizes that you can have a lot of fun with. They can have different segments so maybe a different guest comes in and so this is a segment on such and such an aspect of what it is that you're selling in this particular webinar livecast and it can be a mixture of celebrity guests as well as testimonials. So a great way to make this work is either in a traditional Jeff Walker style launch or you could do this when

## [03:02 - 06:05]

you're having several different JV partners all come together to do this and have those partners the people who are mailing to support you in this come on the livecast and then they mail to their people saying hey I'm going on Don's livecast in 15 minutes come watch. Then you can also put in other guests so for example people who have taken your program and have had great success so you can bring them on they become a testimonial guest they're telling about their results using your product and these multi hour events can also involve multiple mailings during the event to announce a different segment coming up in 15 minutes and so instead of just sending out one email saying hey the webinar is starting you could send out three or four of them throughout the livecast talking about different segments because for the next hour we're going to be talking about this aspect of the business or this aspect of the transformation and how to make that work in your particular life and as I said live guests can either be in person which is by far the ideal way to do it or you can bring them in via Skype. Another nice touch is if each celebrity guest offers a bonus on the livecast so each one of them as they come in they can say hey I've got something special and so this is much like the testimonials and bonuses that are offered on a JV webinar where the JV host offers a bonus to their buyers to buy. Guests on your livecast can do a similar thing where each one of them offers a special bonus but those bonuses then go to everyone who buys and that can create a massive bonus package that has huge value and huge desirability in the eyes of potential buyers. Now we talked about the multi-gast, multi-hour kind of livecast. You can also do a single person livecast which is just you. Now it could go on and be multi-hour in fact Jeff Walker recently ran a launch that was basically four live casts of just him working with a big flip chart behind him so no fancy slides and things drawing pictures on it to illustrate what he's trying to do and it was an incredibly successful launch. Now that particular set of livecast was

## [06:05 - 09:10]

four live casts as I mentioned he was working from a flip chart live to camera he did have some pre-recorded video testimonials and some other pre-recorded segments to give him a break so he could take a drink or just sit back and breathe for a second during the actual live cast and he also little secret thing he did a super secret segment at the end and so people stayed on just to see this super secret segment where he where an additional principle was taught and it was incredibly powerful. It was all presented as part of his three-part launch so no offer was actually made until the fourth live cast in this in this series and the remarkable thing is whereas his pre-launch content in a traditional launch might be 20 to 30 minutes long these were multiple hours two and three hours and people not only stayed but they bought it was an incredibly interesting way of doing a launch basically turning a launch into a series of live webinars live casts. Now I will tell you this is not for everyone it's much more complex you got cameras you got lighting you've got broadband you've got potentially guests you've got you being on camera for potentially a couple of hours you've got all kinds of things that could go wrong and this is literally live TV and everyone's seen bad things happen on live TV and so you only want to go to this trouble if you're going to have a large audience there but this actually can be the thing that creates that live audience and so it's a chicken and egg do you not do it because you don't have a big enough audience or do you do it so you will have the big enough audience to do it. I do caution you whether you run the multi-person one or the single person one make sure that you regularly sell with regular offers interspersed throughout your live cast. The exception is the first three live casts that Jeff did because that was following his product launch formula model and so they're not designed to sell at that stage but if you're doing one that is an entire sales event all in one live cast make sure that you're regularly putting in sales offers interspersed throughout that so that someone doesn't have to stay online for two or three hours

## [09:10 - 09:50]

to be able to hear your offer they're getting it about once every 40 minutes or so they hear about your product they hear about your offer they have a chance to buy then you go back out into more of the information entertainment mode and then you 40 40 50 minutes later drop back into offer mode again so that people are regularly getting exposed to that offer hearing it repeatedly yes or for the first time if they just joined since the last one but giving being given the chance to buy
